agentMessage.excerpt:
Review checklist → pass
✓  Build completed — the implementor finished in 24 round(s)
✓  Change is visible — 3 file(s) changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, README.md, docs/API.md
✓  Changed the deliverable's target files — expected ≥1 of: README.md, docs/API.md, docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md
✓  Verification passed — the project verify command was GREEN
✓  Work committed — committed locally on the build branch
0.90  Implements the approved plan — Created docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md with canonical provider ID table, stability contract (immutable, never reassigned), deprecation policy (5-step process), changelog expectation. Linked from README.md and docs/API.md. No code changes. Minor deviation: acceptance criteria called for deprecation policy covering 'renames with both old and new IDs served concurrently'; the delivered doc takes an 'immutable IDs' stance and frames deprecation around provider unavailability rather than renames. The mechanism (deprecated ID stays valid on read paths, new ID can be added) effectively supports the same outcome.
1.00  Minimal and scoped — Only 3 documentation files changed: new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, one-line link addition in README.md, small 'See Also' section in docs/API.md. No source code changes, no unrelated churn. Exactly matches the plan's scope.
1.00  No test-gaming / faking — Documentation is substantive (~63 lines, well-structured with 5 sections including a full provider table). No placeholder/empty text. No tests were modified — verify command passed on a docs-only diff. Real, actionable content for integrators.
1.00  Safe to advance — Documentation is a policy statement, not executable code. No security or correctness risk. Links are valid relative paths. Deprecation behavior described (400 with provider_deprecated error code) is reasonable. No misleading or dangerous claims.
0.92  Judge confidence — at or above the confidence bar
